parsed_review.summary: CP00-946 is a clean, descriptor-only RP30 P06 pack bridging SensitiveGuard tail rows (RP30.P06.M01.S08-S13) into PayrollRestriction foundation rows (RP30.P06.M02.S01-S04). The pack-level claim is narrow and honest: manifest.json declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, descriptor_only=true, with the only positive flag being hrx_sensitive_guard_payroll_restriction_bridge_descriptor_verified. That claim is fully supported by static inspection: (1) the registry binding (registry.js:916-931) and requirements (registry.js:3551-3605) match the upstream plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:36), the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, the review baseline, and the source microphases (M01=SensitiveGuard x6, M02=PayrollRestriction x4 = 10 implementation units); (2) the generated descriptor (registry.js:10831-10910) and domain rows (createHrxDomainRows registry.js:3656-3969) hold every runtime/record-write/decision-emission/real-data/permission-bypass/payroll-calculation flag false and keep sensitive_hr_guard_required/h30_review_only/c30_read_only/human_final_approval_required true, with entity_name mapping correctly to SensitiveGuard/PayrollRestriction and HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION spread in; (3) the coverage/descriptor/contract validators (validators.js:8527-8726) enforce all of these, and the validate script (validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs) chains CP897->CP946 bindings and exits non-zero on error; (4) the committed contract artifact hrx-people-contract.json is consistent (current_pack/latest_pack/latest_projection CP00-946; CP946 capabilities and safety gates present at top level; validation.valid true); (5) tests (model.test.js:2424-2514) assert binding, distribution counts, descriptor closure, and validate the real contract file. The authority boundary is respected: Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not final approver, Hermes is used only as a deterministic control-plane review packet (emits_runtime_receipt=false, deterministic_only=true) and not embedded product runtime, and the pack asserts neither runtime nor enterprise trust, so blocks_goal_closeout is false. The PENDING state of adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, command-evidence.json (hermes_gate pending), and claude-review-result.json is the expected pre-review state, not a defect. Provenance note: as a read-only reviewer I did NOT re-execute the validation ladder; test/validation-green status is taken from command-evidence.json exit codes (5 HRX-scoped commands, all 0) plus my static reading of internally consistent code and artifacts. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; two P3 notes (stale manifest commit message; validation ladder not yet captured).

findings.title: Manifest commit_policy.message references CP00-945 instead of CP00-946
findings.details: The CP00-946 manifest's commit_policy.message is "Close CP00-945 HRX permission sensitive guard bridge pack" - a stale carry-over from the previous pack. Every prior manifest names its own pack (cp00-944/manifest.json -> "Close CP00-944 ...", cp00-945/manifest.json -> "Close CP00-945 ..."), so the convention is that commit_policy.message describes the current pack's closeout commit; CP00-946 deviates. Impact is low: validate-closeout-pack.mjs:250 only checks commit_policy.required===true and never validates .message, and a grep of scripts/ shows no tool reads commit_policy.message, so it does not break the validation ladder. Recent git history (every "Close CP00-XXX" commit matches its pack) indicates the actual closeout commit message is constructed independently of this field, so the stale value is effectively inert. Flagged for artifact accuracy/audit-trail hygiene.
findings.recommendation: Update commit_policy.message in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-946/manifest.json to "Close CP00-946 HRX sensitive guard payroll restriction bridge pack" (matching the per-pack convention) before the closeout commit, so the manifest does not duplicate the CP00-945 commit title and remains accurate to the pack it closes.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; inert metadata not consumed by any validator or commit script. Correct at/before the closeout commit, else defer with rationale.

findings.title: Broader closeout validation ladder not yet captured in command-evidence.json
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only 5 HRX-scoped commands (node --check x3, node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js, npm run rp30:hrx:validate), all exit_code 0, with status 'pending_final_validation', gate_outcome 'pending_final_validation', and hermes_gate 'pending'. The 10 closeout validation commands listed in the review prompt (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run spec:requirements:validate, npm run weighted:validate, npm run fullplan:validate, npm run goal:closeout:validate, npm run rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) are not yet evidenced here. This is the expected pre-closeout state, and as a read-only reviewer I cannot execute them; the descriptor-only claim itself is independently supported by the consistent registry/validators/contract/tests inspected. Flagged only for provenance transparency.
findings.recommendation: The pack closeout runner should execute and capture the full validation ladder (including git diff --check) with exit 0 and run the Hermes gate before flipping manifest/included_units status from in_progress to production_ready and writing the single valid review receipt; ensure command-evidence/Hermes gate reflect the completed ladder rather than 'pending'.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; expected pre-closeout state. Deferred to the pack closeout runner at final validation.
